title: Ceasefire Weather

mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: A day of fragile relief rather than resolution: Israel and Iran both halted their attacks while Tehran warned it could resume over southern Lebanon — and even in the pause a strike on Tyre killed five — as oil eased toward $93 and the AI trade carried markets up on OpenAI's IPO filing, while Mindanao's quake toll climbed to 37, Peru's runoff hung on a hair, and the DRC's Ebola emergency passed 90 deaths.

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano, vibraphone (VCSL), RolandTR808
pattern: stack(s("bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ bd ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~, ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh").bank("RolandTR808").gain(0.4).hpf(80).room(0.18), note("d2 ~ ~ a1 c2 ~ ~ ~ g1 ~ ~ d2 a1 ~ ~ ~").s("sine").attack(0.02).release(0.7).lpf(360).gain(0.46), note("<[d3,f3,a3] [c3,e3,g3] [g2,b2,d3] [a2,c3,e3]>").s("piano").gain(0.46).room(0.42).rsize(5).pan(0.47).slow(2), note("<[d4,a4,c5,e5] [c4,g4,b4,d5] [g3,d4,f4,a4] [a3,e4,g4,c5]>").s("sawtooth").attack(2.4).decay(0.8).sustain(0.45).release(4).lpf(sine.range(420,1300).slow(18)).gain(0.07).room(0.6).rsize(8), note("d5 ~ ~ e5 ~ c5 ~ ~ a4 ~ ~ ~ g4 ~ f4 ~ a4 ~ ~ c5 ~ ~ b4 ~ a4 ~ ~ ~ g4 ~ ~ ~").s("piano").gain(0.36).room(0.46).pan(0.53).delay(0.24).delaytime(0.42).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.13).pan(0.62).lpf(1500)), note("~ ~ a5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ f5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ d5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ e5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~").s("vibraphone").gain(0.22).room(0.5).rsize(5).pan(0.4).delay(0.2).delaytime(0.5))

lines: Both sides set their hands down this morning, the sky kept its missiles at home,
but the quiet came with a warning — one wrong night and the fire comes back.
Over Tyre the pause didn't hold; five gone and the medics among them,
so don't call it peace yet, call it weather, the kind that could turn in a crack.
The ticker likes a ceasefire, oil slides and the AI money climbs,
but Mindanao still counts its dead, and Peru holds its breath on a thread.
Ebola creeps past ninety in the Congo, Sudan turns into its fourth year,
while New Zealand draws a line round the penguins — small mercies, like always, instead.
It's ceasefire weather, not the all-clear, a calm we don't quite trust,
the guns gone quiet but leaning, the markets up on a maybe.
Hold the day the way it is — half relief and half the rust,
the kind of breath you take in slow, in case you have to hold it for me.
